justification: All pass criteria met: SLA math clearly identified (99.9% Onfido vs 99.95% team SLA, quantified as ~26 min/year gap), multiple mitigation options recommended (circuit breaker with async fallback queue, pending_verification degraded mode, async KYC retry), circuit breaker gap explicitly called out as the critical gap, health endpoint/Onfido coupling noted in monitoring coverage, and verdict is NOT READY with escalation to MJ for architectural decision.

observations.text: SLA math is precise and quantified — not just flagged but explained with the 0.05% gap translated to ~26 minutes/year
Circuit breaker absence identified as the single most critical gap, with specific implementation guidance (5 failures in 30s → open for 60s)
Comprehensive failure mode enumeration covering full outage, latency degradation, rate limiting, malformed responses, thread pool exhaustion, and network partition
Recommends async KYC with pending_verification state as standard fintech practice — exactly the right mitigation
Health endpoint coupling issue identified — notes /health checking Onfido reachability but flags missing alerting and the implication for service restarts
Escalations are well-structured, routing the architectural decision (degraded-mode contract) to MJ appropriately

Health endpoint issue could be more explicit about the orchestrator restart risk — it's mentioned in monitoring_coverage but not called out as a distinct failure mode where Onfido downtime triggers unnecessary pod restarts

observations.text: SLA math is precise: 99.9% = 8.76 hours/year vs 99.95% = 4.38 hours/year, with 0.05% deficit clearly stated
Circuit breaker gap identified with specific implementation guidance (open after 5 failures in 30s, half-open after 60s)
Multiple fallback options: pending_verification async state, queue-based retry, limited platform access during verification
Health endpoint coupling to Onfido reachability noted in monitoring coverage
Thorough failure mode analysis covering outage, latency spike, rate limiting, malformed payload, and thundering herd scenarios
Verdict is NOT READY with clear escalation to architecture for the SLA decision
